interpretations.claim: Mutual mistrust and suspicion existed before the flight.
interpretations.boundary: Must be paired with I-S11-002 and cannot be used as a deterministic condition or repair gate.

interpretations.claim: Substantial confidence in Louis also remained before the flight.
interpretations.boundary: Must be paired with I-S11-001 and cannot be generalized into a nationwide measurement.

cards.title: France is in revolution
cards.standardText: France has been undergoing revolutionary political change since 1789. Political authority is being remade and contested.
cards.reducedText: France has been in revolution since 1789. Political authority is changing and contested.

cards.title: France still has a king
cards.standardText: Louis XVI remains king in June 1791. France is not yet a republic.
cards.reducedText: Louis XVI is still king. France is not yet a republic.

cards.title: The king's power is being limited
cards.standardText: A written constitution is still being finished. It will define and limit the king's powers.
cards.reducedText: A constitution is being written to define and limit the king's powers.

glossary.definition: A system in which a monarch remains head of state while a constitution defines and limits political power.

cards.title: The royal family is in Paris
cards.standardText: The royal family lives at the Tuileries in Paris. On 18 April, a crowd and National Guard stopped Louis from departing for Saint-Cloud.
cards.reducedText: The royal family lives at the Tuileries in Paris. On 18 April, a crowd and National Guard stopped Louis from leaving for Saint-Cloud.

cards.title: Trust is an open question
cards.standardText: Many people still support monarchy, but trust between the crown and revolutionary politics is fragile. This is a contested interpretation, not a nationwide measurement.
cards.reducedText: Many people support monarchy, but trust is fragile. This is an interpretation, not a measured national fact.

glossary.definition: An explanation or meaning that historians or historical actors can reasonably dispute.

cards.title: The royal family leaves secretly
cards.standardText: On the night of 20-21 June 1791, the royal family secretly leaves the Tuileries in Paris.
cards.reducedText: The royal family secretly leaves the Tuileries in Paris on the night of 20-21 June 1791.
cards.closingPrompt: You are not expected to know what happens next. That is the case you will investigate.
